name: eager-l3
description: RAG eager L3 extraction Canary suite. Started as a partial port of `Qualia/scripts/smoke-eager-l3.mjs` (Phase M1, 2026-05-25). The Move-2 Canary `Reload` action (added 2026-05-25 to `QualiaBridgeAgent.cs` per the LLM observability roadmap) unblocked the reload-dependent fixtures; Move 3 (2026-05-27, Phase 3 eager-L3 plan) added the progress-badge fixture + made all sweep-dependent fixtures explicitly enable the `compute.rag.eager-l3` persona via `__canarySetPersonaEnabled`. Order is cheapest-first: `reload-smoke` (foundational infra check — verifies the bridge agent's Reload action preserves localStorage + re-establishes hooks) -> `no-provider-noop` (silent-no-op path per ADR 0031) -> `progress-badge` (asserts the EagerExtractionProgressBadge becomes visible during a live sweep; ~10s) -> `cold-launch` (sweep enqueues N > 0 with provider seeded; ~95s) -> `warm-launch` (idempotency via cache-skip + skip-on-empty-derived; ~30s). The cold/warm pair has an intentional dependency: cold-launch populates the on-disk behavior cache at `examples/sample/.qualia.rag/behavior-cache.json` which warm-launch's skip-detection relies on. Provider-swap fixture queued for Phase 2 v2 (needs the maxContentBytes ceiling shipped + the sweep telemetry surface so the assertion can read which model fired which extraction).
